expectation: A member (or guest) visiting an open public forum space expects: clear orientation (what is this space, who's here, is it active), obvious primary actions (reply, follow, sort), recency/status signals (timestamps, activity counts), and graceful empty states with CTAs when needed. For guests: "Log in to post" button. For members: "New Post" button. Empty states are handled via the empty-state partial with icon, message, and optional CTA. Pagination via "Load More" button. expectation: A member visiting the new post composer expects to find: clear orientation about what space they're posting to, obvious form fields with helpful hints, a clear primary action to submit their post, a cancel option to abort, and feedback on submission success/failure without dead ends.
present:
The new-post view renders:
1. Breadcrumb navigation (Home > Space Title > "New Topic/Question/Status/Idea")
2. Page heading ("New Topic", "Ask a Question", "Share an Idea", or "New Status" depending on space type)
3. Subtitle confirming "Posting in [Space Name]"
4. Error alert area (hidden by default, shown when submitError state is truthy)
5. Form with dynamically-shown fields based on space type:
   - Title input (auto-focused, required for non-feed spaces, optional placeholder text matching space type)
   - Tags input (optional, comma-separated)
   - Prefix select (optional, only if space has prefixes enabled)
   - Content editor (WYSIWYG editor or textarea, required)
   - Private checkbox (logged-in members only)
   - Scheduler panel (hidden by default, shown when user selects "Schedule..." from publish menu)
   - CAPTCHA widget (renders if configured)
   - Publish mode dropdown (if show_publish_menu=true): "Publish now", "Save as draft", "Schedule..."
   - Cancel button (always shown, links back to space)
   - Submit button (type-aware: "Post Question", "Submit Idea", "Post Status", "Post Topic")

Non-member access: redirects to space page (not shown here, but line 80 in class-template-loader.php enforces this).
Anonymous access: redirects to login with redirect_to preserved.

On form submission (composePost action):
- Title validation (required for non-feed spaces)
- Content validation (both HTML and plain-text required)
- Tags collected and split
- Schedule fields validated (date + time both required if draft status + scheduler shown)
- CAPTCHA token collected
- API call to POST /spaces/{spaceId}/posts
- Success: redirect to post view or show toast (for drafts/pending)
- Error: display error message in submitError alert + toast notification

gaps.gap: The error alert div uses initial `hidden` attribute but relies on `data-wp-bind--hidden="!state.submitError"` to toggle visibility. If Interactivity API hydration is delayed or fails, the error message could be written to the DOM but remain invisible to the user, creating a silent failure where the form doesn't respond to the user's submission attempt.
gaps.evidence: templates/views/new-post.php:85-90 — the div has both `hidden` and `data-wp-bind--hidden` binding, but if JS fails to hydrate, the binding never executes and the hidden attribute persists even when state.submitError is truthy.

gaps.gap: The similar-topics panel is hidden initially and only appears if the title input has focus and JavaScript fires the search. There's no loading state indicator or explanation for why the panel might be empty, so if the search returns no results or loads slowly, the user has no feedback about what's happening.
gaps.evidence: templates/partials/compose-fields.php:101-111 — the jt-similar div is marked `hidden` with no visible placeholder or 'searching...' message. Users may not understand why it's blank or whether it's still loading.

gaps.gap: The form does not display inline validation feedback for individual fields (e.g., 'Title is required' under the title input before submit). Users only see errors in the top alert after a failed submission attempt, requiring them to scroll back up or hunt for the error message.
gaps.evidence: compose-fields.php has no `aria-invalid`, `data-error`, or per-field error message containers. All feedback flows to the centralized `jt-form-error` alert (line 85-90 in new-post.php).

gaps.gap: When a user selects 'Save as draft' or 'Schedule', the form behavior diverges: drafts show a toast and return to the composer (submit button text changes to 'Save Draft'), while scheduled posts are subject to undocumented validation (date + time required when draft status + scheduler shown). If date is missing, a toast fires but the submitError alert is not updated, so the user may not see the error in the alert area they're watching.
gaps.evidence: assets/js/view.js:2512-2517 — when collecting schedule on draft status and no date is provided, a toast is shown and the function returns early, but state.submitError is never set. User expects unified error feedback but gets only a transient toast notification.

gaps.gap: The submit button text dynamically updates (e.g., 'Posting...' during submission via data-wp-text binding), but if the Interactivity API fails to initialize, the button text stays frozen at the original label and the user has no loading indicator, making it unclear whether their click registered.
gaps.evidence: templates/partials/compose-fields.php:246-247 — the button binds data-wp-text="state.submitLabel" and data-wp-bind--disabled="state.isSubmitting". If hydration fails, both disabled state and text update are silent no-ops.

expectation: A trusted member expects a clear form to create a new community space with obvious field guidance, form submission feedback, and a clear escape route if they change their mind.
present: Two-column layout (form + sidebar) with breadcrumb, H1 title, 8 form fields (title/description/type/visibility/join-policy/category/cover-image/icon), help text on several fields, icon picker with search, cover image uploader, submit+cancel buttons, and an error display box that reveals on API failure.
states_checked: Qualified user (form shown, all fields visible) and non-qualified user (empty-state shown) states both confirmed via template conditionals. Success state (redirect to /s/:slug/) happens client-side. Error state (error box unhidden with message) happens on REST API failure.

gaps.gap: Form fields lack visible inline validation or character counters despite having maxlength constraints. Title field allows 120 chars, description 280 chars, but users see nothing when hitting limits — the browser silently prevents further input without feedback.
gaps.evidence: new-space.php:70,76 — maxlength="120" and maxlength="280" with no counter or focus hint. CSS shows .jt-form-help exists (jetonomy.css:407) but it never acknowledges remaining char budget.

gaps.gap: Cover image upload failure does NOT block form submission. If upload fails (network error, unsupported format), user sees error in the status element, clears it after 2s (new-space.js:63), then can submit form without a cover image anyway. No UI prevents accidental submission without realizing upload failed.
gaps.evidence: new-space.js:50-64 — status clears automatically after 2s; form submission (line 86) doesn't re-validate cover_image on submit. User might think 'Uploading...' succeeded and move forward, only to realize later the cover was never attached.

gaps.gap: Description field is labeled as optional (no asterisk) but no explicit '(optional)' text. Contrast with title field which has a visible asterisk. New space creators may not immediately grasp the priority difference.
gaps.evidence: new-space.php:69 vs 75 — title has 'span class="jt-required"' asterisk; description omits it. Standard form convention is to mark optional fields when required fields are marked, or vice versa.

gaps.gap: Icon picker 'Show more icons' button has no clear affordance that it reveals additional hidden icons. User might not realize 16 base icons aren't the full set until they click 'Show more'. No tooltip, badge, or label hints the button expands hidden content.
gaps.evidence: icon-picker.php:240-242 — button says 'Show more icons' but no count or hint of how many are hidden; extended icons only become visible onClick via data-jt-icon-extended.

expectation: A member visiting a space's member list expects to see all members with clear role indicators, manage role/ban options (if admin), and understand the community makeup at a glance with no dead ends or unactionable states.

gaps.gap: Ban button disables after click but provides no visual feedback when the ban succeeds. Row opacity changes to 0.5 and a text 'Banned' label is appended via JS, but there's no CSS styling for .jt-member-banned-note—it renders as plain unstyled text inline. Admins don't know if the ban's visual state is complete or if something failed.
gaps.evidence: space-members.js:226 creates span.jt-member-banned-note with text 'Banned', but assets/css/jetonomy.css has zero `.jt-member-banned-note` rule. Row style opacity=0.5 is inline-only and the appended text blends with the row without visual distinction.

gaps.gap: Member reputation scores only display if the member has a user profile record (jt_user_profiles). If a member joined but never earned reputation, no rep score renders—leaving ambiguity about whether it's 0, unknown, or a data gap. No '0 rep' fallback or visual marker.

When a space admin changes a member's role and the server returns a 400 error, the error message renders below the dropdown in a plain

tag with no visual distinction—just red text on the row background. On crowded member lists, the error can appear disconnected from the action that triggered it.

gaps.gap: List mode uses fixed-width percentages for columns (Date: 22%, Edited By: 22%, Diff: 12%) but does not include margin/padding guidance in the CSS, so table layout may look cramped or unaligned on smaller screens. No responsive behavior is documented.
gaps.evidence: class-revisions-list-table.php:83-86 — hardcoded style='width: 22%' inline; no media queries or responsive handling mentioned in admin.css

gaps.gap: When a post/reply is deleted after revisions are recorded, the detail view link will still exist in list mode but clicking it will show an empty detail page (no revisions found, or confusing title like 'Post #123: (no title)'). No warning or indication that the object has been deleted.

expectation: An owner persona expects to see forum import sources, check their status, start a migration with one click, understand progress in real-time, and recover from interruptions — with clear next steps at every state and no silent failures.
present: Template renders three states: (1) empty state showing "No Forum Data Detected" with three disabled plugin-source buttons and help text; (2) populated state showing available sources as cards with import history, stats, and action buttons (primary for first import, secondary for re-import); (3) progress UI with multi-phase pipeline (Forums → Topics → Replies → Profiles → Finalize), progress bar, and status text (initially hidden). A warning banner at the top recommends database backup before importing.

gaps.gap: Empty state provides no path forward — no links to install source plugins or documentation. Disabled buttons offer no affordance to guide owners on next steps, leaving them stuck.
gaps.evidence: import.php:27-50 — 'No Forum Data Detected' with disabled buttons and static text 'install one of these plugins and create some content' but no href, no docs link, no setup guide.

gaps.gap: Results container is empty and never populated — no template for displaying import summary, error details, or failed records. Owners cannot see success count or error list after import completes or fails.
gaps.evidence:
import.php:195 — '
' is defined but never populated by the template. Class_Import_Handler::ajax_import_batch() (class-import-handler.php:114–124) returns success/failure via JSON but template has no conditional to render results or errors.

gaps.gap: No visibility into import errors — Importer class logs errors (class-importer.php:111–117) but template has no error display. If 50 records fail, owner has no in-UI feedback; must check logs or browser console.
gaps.evidence: import.php has no conditional block for rendering $importer->errors or an error summary. Template assumes 100% success.

gaps.gap: Race condition in resume state — if an import is still running and owner refreshes, page may show both 'Resume Import' button and active progress bar simultaneously, causing confusion about which action is safe to take.
gaps.evidence: import.php:57 checks '$has_resume = !empty($resume_state)' but does not check if import is currently 'running' status (line 58 checks $is_running only for display, not button logic). Template renders resume button if has_resume is true regardless of running status.

gaps.gap: No elapsed-time indicator for in-progress imports — template shows resume start time but not how long import has been running or ETA, so owner cannot gauge if import is stuck or proceeding normally.
gaps.evidence: import.php:115–123 displays 'Started: %3$s' for resumed imports but not for active progress. jetonomy-import-status (line 188–191) shows only percent and message, no elapsed/total time.

states_checked: Empty state: Confirmed — triggers when $available is empty AND $resume_state is empty (line 27). Populated state: Confirmed — renders when $available has entries (line 51+). Progress state: Confirmed — initially hidden with style="display:none" (line 173), shown via JS during active import. Error/loading states: Not found — no conditional branches for errors or loading in the template. Role-gated access: Confirmed via class-admin.php add_submenu_page() capability 'jetonomy_manage_settings' but not shown in template itself.

expectation: An owner visiting the Jetonomy Settings admin page expects clear navigation across all tabs (General, Permissions, Email, Appearance, SEO, Anti-Spam, Integrations if BuddyPress is active, Free vs Pro if free, License if Pro), fully-populated settings forms with obvious save buttons, empty-state guidance where content is missing, and feedback on successful saves.
present: Sidebar navigation with icon-labeled tabs, form-based settings with grouped cards for each feature area, Pro upsell blocks, success/error message handling via WordPress settings_errors(), and a unified submit button at the bottom of most tabs. Tab content is conditionally rendered based on plugin state (free/pro) and active plugins (BuddyPress). The Free-vs-Pro tab shows a hero section with extension grid; License tab delegates to Pro via do_action hook.

gaps.gap: License tab shows empty card body when Pro is active. Only a card header renders, with content delegated via `do_action( 'jetonomy_admin_license_tab_content' )` on line 1240. If Pro plugin doesn't fire this hook or renders nothing, owner sees a titled card with blank content area — no loading indicator, no fallback CTA (e.g., 'License content not available'), no status signal. Owner hesitates: is the content broken, still loading, or intentionally empty?
gaps.evidence: includes/admin/views/settings.php:1233-1241; do_action() hook with no fallback rendering

gaps.gap: Integrations tab has no content guard for missing BuddyPress. If BP Groups is not active, the Integrations tab link does NOT appear in sidebar (lines 58-62) — good. BUT if a user manually navigates to `&tab=integrations` via URL without BP Groups active, the page renders blank main content area (line 1243 condition fails, nothing renders). Owner sees a sidebar with no highlighted tab, a blank main area, and no message explaining why. Expectation: either hide the URL path or show 'This integration requires BuddyPress Groups'.
gaps.evidence: includes/admin/views/settings.php:58-62 (conditional tab creation), 1243 (conditional content render); no fallback else clause

gaps.gap: Free-vs-Pro tab positioned as primary tab but has no submit button. Tab is in `$jt_primary_tabs` array (line 20) and uses the main form wrapper (line 119), creating visual/structural expectation of a saveable settings form. But line 1296-1298 explicitly excludes 'free-vs-pro' from submit_button() rendering. Owner sees a form-like card layout with an upgrade CTA but no save affordance — minor UX friction since upgrades redirect externally, but inconsistent with other tabs.
gaps.evidence: includes/admin/views/settings.php:20 (free-vs-pro in primary tabs), 1296-1298 (submit_button exclusion for free-vs-pro)

gaps.gap: Integrations form has redundant settings_fields() call inside nested form. The outer form (line 119) wraps all primary tabs with `settings_fields( 'jetonomy_settings' )`. The Integrations section (line 1245) has its own inner `<form>` with `settings_fields( 'jetonomy_integrations' )`, meaning two form elements are nested (invalid HTML). Owner submits and only the inner form posts; if an integrations setting is saved, the outer primary-tab fields are silently ignored. Risk of lost edits if both tabs are edited in one session.
gaps.evidence: includes/admin/views/settings.php:119 (outer form open), 1245-1246 (inner form with different settings_fields), 1281 (inner form close); outer form never closes until line 1299

gaps.gap: Test Email button on Email tab provides no visible feedback while sending. Button text changes to 'Sending...' (admin-settings.js:93) but no inline spinner, toast, or visual loading indicator. Async AJAX completes and alerts result (line 99) — but if JS fails silently or alert is dismissed, owner has no status. Button re-enables after POST completes, but no transient success message persists on the page if they navigate away.
gaps.evidence: assets/js/admin-settings.js:88-102; button.disabled + text-swap, then alertFn() modal; no inline feedback element

gaps.gap: Email template Reset button visibility logic is JS-dependent. Button displays only if an override exists in DB (line 679-685 renders `display:none` inline if no override). After user edits a row and saves, JS doesn't toggle button visibility until next page reload. Owner edits 'welcome' template, saves page, Reset button still hidden (until refresh), creating confusion: 'Is my edit saved? Why can't I reset?'
gaps.evidence: includes/admin/views/settings.php:679-687 (inline display:none, visibility toggled by JS); assets/js/admin-settings.js line 127 sets display:none after reset, but no toggle after save

states_checked: Populated: General, Permissions, Email, Appearance, SEO, Antispam tabs render with full form fields and defaults. Free-vs-Pro tab (free plugin only) renders upsell hero + extension grid. License tab (Pro only) renders card header with delegated hook content — EMPTY if Pro hook doesn't fire. expectation: An owner launching Jetonomy for the first time expects a clear, guided setup that establishes a working community space in 3 steps, with obvious next actions at each stage, clear error recovery, and visible progress. They expect to understand what happens after completion and how to access their new community.
present: A 3-step wizard with: Step 1 (Basics) - URL slug + community type radio buttons, Step 2 (First Space) - category + space name + description with sample-data toggle, Step 3 (Success) - completion screen with "Visit Community" and "Go to Dashboard" CTAs + next-steps tips. Progress indicator with visual state changes (active → done transitions). Form validation on slug + category/space names. Error messages in red banners. Loading spinner on buttons. URL preview live-updates as slug changes.
states_checked: Empty/initial (Step 1 loads pre-filled with 'community' slug and 'General' category), loaded (form fields populated with defaults), error (validation on missing slug on step 1→2, missing category/space names on step 2→3, shows red error box), populated/success (Step 3 success state), mobile (390px viewport for responsive testing). Role-gated by 'manage_options' cap at setup.php include; nonce-protected AJAX actions checked via check_ajax_referer().

gaps.gap: Step 3 success screen offers 'Visit Community' (external link, new tab) and 'Go to Dashboard' but provides no inline confirmation that setup is truly complete or data was saved. An owner could click 'Visit Community', see a 404 or empty forum, and assume the setup failed.
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.php lines 208-216: both CTAs are links without success feedback. The AJAX success callback just calls showStep(3). No message like 'Your base_slug and space have been saved' appears on the final screen. Line 86 in handler.php sets update_option( 'jetonomy_setup_complete', true ) but the frontend never displays this confirmation.

gaps.gap: Step 1 validation only checks if slug is non-empty; it does not validate that the slug is URL-safe or warn about reserved WordPress paths (/wp-admin, /wp-content, etc.) that could conflict. An owner could enter 'wp-admin' or 'admin' and create an unreachable community.
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.js lines 76-82: validation only checks !slug, then showStep(2). The sanitizeSlug() function on lines 54-56 removes invalid chars but doesn't pre-validate against WP reserved routes. Handler sanitizes as sanitize_title() on line 38 but no conflict check.

gaps.gap: Step 2 offers a 'Create sample data' button (line 185-189) but does not explain what sample data includes or how many posts/users it creates. A new owner might not realize they are about to populate their forum with fake content, or they might expect it to be reversible from the UI (cleanup requires the Dashboard, not the wizard).
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.php line 187: button label says '2 categories, 6 spaces across all four types, ~12 posts' but doesn't clarify these are dummy posts. handler.php line 138 returns only 'Sample community created...' message. No warning that this can only be undone from admin dashboard (via cleanup AJAX action jetonomy_cleanup_sample_data).

gaps.gap: Step 2 'Create sample data' button succeeds silently with only a brief success message. If an owner then clicks 'Create Space' instead, or refreshes, they may not realize they already seeded the community and create duplicate data structures.
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.js lines 158-165: the sample-data AJAX shows no before/after state distinction. Both 'Create Space' and 'Create sample data' buttons advance to step 3 (showStep(3)), making it unclear which path was taken.

gaps.gap: If the setup wizard is re-run after initial setup (e.g., after a plugin update), Step 1 repopulates with the stored base_slug but does not warn the owner that changing it will break existing URLs and member links. An owner might think they can 'edit' the slug without consequence.
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.php lines 10-11: base_slug is loaded from options and pre-filled in the input (line 106). setup-wizard.js has no warning when re-running the wizard. handler.php lines 37-40 preserve existing base_slug if empty, but the UI gives no hint that re-running has consequences.

gaps.gap: The 'Next Steps' tips on Step 3 (lines 220-227) include 'Import existing content from bbPress or wpForo' but do not provide a link or instructions to access the import feature. An owner interested in migrating content must navigate the dashboard manually to find it.
gaps.evidence: setup-wizard.php lines 221-226: the tips are plain-text list items, no links to admin pages for customization, import, or member management.

expectation: An owner should see community health metrics at a glance with clear status indicators, understand whether data is loading/empty/error, and have obvious next steps for taking action.
present: A dashboard with stat cards (Posts, Replies, Active Users, Votes), activity chart, top spaces, top contributors, content health, and moderation metrics. Values initialize to "--" placeholders; tables show "Loading..." text; data populates via REST API calls; permission errors and fetch errors surface as admin notices.

gaps.gap: Loading state lacks visual feedback: bare 'Loading...' text in tables/chart provides no progress indicator, spinner, or skeleton. Owner cannot tell if it's stalled or responding.
gaps.evidence: dashboard.php lines 108, 147, 166, 184 and admin-analytics.js line 118 render plain text 'Loading...' with no CSS animation or visual pulse. CSS .jtp-loading only has text-align/padding/color (pro-admin.css:479).

gaps.gap: No indication of which range/filter is active on initial load besides the 'active' button class. Owner sees dashes everywhere and doesn't immediately know the data is for '30 Days' (the default).
gaps.evidence: dashboard.php line 66 sets 30d button as active, but no text label or caption explains 'Showing: Last 30 days' alongside the stat cards. Admin must read the button.

gaps.gap: Fetch error handling is vague: generic 'Could not load analytics' message doesn't explain why (network, timeout, server error) or suggest a retry action. Only permission errors are differentiated.
gaps.evidence: admin-analytics.js lines 24-39: showFetchError() renders 'Could not load analytics. Try refreshing the page.' with status code appended, but no detail on cause. No distinct messaging for 500 vs 503 vs timeout.

gaps.gap: If one REST endpoint fails (e.g., moderation endpoint returns 500), other endpoints still populate, leaving the dashboard partially filled with a silent gap. Owner sees some data and assumes the page loaded fully, missing a section.
gaps.evidence: admin-analytics.js lines 274–303 fetchApi() calls are independent; loadAll() makes 5 parallel fetch calls without awaiting all to succeed. One failure shows error notice but others continue to render.

gaps.gap: Chart empty state shows 'No data for this period' but does not clearly indicate whether that's zero activity (healthy for a brand-new forum) vs. a problem. Owner may misinterpret.
gaps.evidence: admin-analytics.js lines 117–119 render the i18n message with no context. No caption like 'Zero posts in this range — space may be inactive.'

gaps.gap: Placeholder '--' dashes in stat cards (Posts, Replies, etc.) are indistinguishable from 'no data' once fully loaded. If a metric genuinely has zero value, owner sees '--' before and after load, creating confusion.
gaps.evidence: dashboard.php lines 118, 123, 128, 133 all initialize to id='jtp-stat-posts' textContent '--'. admin-analytics.js line 276 sets it to formatNumber(d.posts), which resolves to '0' if zero, but visually similar to placeholder.

states_checked:
- **Empty/Loading**: Initial render shows "--" placeholders and "Loading..." in tables; verified JS shows i18n.noData when API returns empty arrays (admin-analytics.js:118, 160, 169, 184, 227).
- **Error**: Permission errors (403) surface as distinct admin notice; generic fetch errors show status code but lack detail (admin-analytics.js:24–39, 52–62).
- **Populated**: Async REST endpoints populate cards, chart, and tables with formatted numbers and styled rows (admin-analytics.js:274–303).
- **Anonymous**: Page gate is 'manage_options' in add_submenu_page (class-extension.php:1301); REST endpoints further gate on jetonomy_view_analytics cap (lines 522, 379). Anonymous users cannot access.
- **Role-gated**: Only admin/users with jetonomy_view_analytics cap can view. No role-based sub-sections (all sections require same cap).

expectation: A site owner expects to see an at-a-glance list of member conversations with quick moderation/purge actions, clear empty states with guidance, and enough context (message preview, last activity) to triage without opening every thread.

Empty list shows "No conversations yet." Table uses standard WordPress widefat styling.

Not found (invalid conversation ID) – returns "Conversation not found." with dead end, no back link. Single-page result – pagination hidden with no affordance showing "this is all of them".

gaps.evidence: class-admin-page.php:253–256 — echo 'Conversation not found' then return, without back link; back link only added on line 259 AFTER successful fetch

gaps.gap: Detail view empty state (conversation with 0 messages) has no message. Table tbody closes silently with no rows, no 'No messages in this conversation' placeholder.

gaps.gap: Conversations list lacks message preview. Columns show Type, Participants, Message Count, Last Activity, but NOT the last message content. Owner cannot triage (e.g., 'is this spam or a legitimate conversation?') without opening each thread.
gaps.evidence: class-admin-page.php:203–237 — table columns are ID, Type, Participants, Messages, Last activity, Actions; no 'Last Message' or preview column; last_message_preview is selected in query (line 188) but never echoed

gaps.gap: Purge button on list view is rendered as inline link style (button-link button-link-delete) rather than a button, reducing visual salience of a destructive action. On detail view (line 319) it's styled as button-link-delete, more prominent.
gaps.evidence: class-admin-page.php:230–231 — 'View' link, then ' | ', then purge_button(..., false) which uses button-link class (line 333) in list context; line 319 passes true, using button button-link-delete classes for stronger visual weight
